1	{
2	  "body": "A few of the root unsatisfiable classes found in the merge product of Uberon and ZFA are caused by what I believe are simple errors that are entirely internal to Uberon (and that are merely revealed by the merge with ZFA).

# uvea

[uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) is said to be [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) the '[anterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001801)', which is defined as “any of the parts of the eye that lie in front of, or ventral to, the lens (inclusive)”.

That doesn’t sound correct as, from what I can see, the uvea clearly appears to extend dorsally relative to the lens (if only by its '[optic choroid](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001776)' part, which is incidentally said to [overlap](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) the '[posterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001802).

The [uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some '[anterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001801)' axiom should be removed – possibly replaced by a relationship to a larger structure, though this may not be needed since there is already axiom stating that [uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) '[contributes to the morphology of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002433)' some '[camera-type eye](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000019)', which should be enough.

# 'future brain vesicle'

'[future brain vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013150)' (no text definition) is classified as an '[open anatomical space](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010064)', ultimately an immaterial anatomical entity.

That does not seem consistent with Uberon’s “habits” of representing most anatomical cavities or spaces with terms representing the (material) structures that make them (plus optionally terms to represent the actual space inside, if it is needed for some reason).

'[future brain vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013150)' is [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) the '[ventricular system of central nervous system](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005281)', which is explicitly defined as “a set of **structures** containing cerebrospinal fluid in the brain” (emphasis mine).

I believe the brain vesicles should be made to be material anatomical structures rather than immaterial anatomical spaces. If needed, we could create a new term “lumen of brain vesicle” to refer to the actual space within those vesicles, similarly to '[lumen of central canal of spinal cord](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009572)'.

# 'scale circulus'

'[scale circulus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002051)' is classified as an immaterial '[anatomical line](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002051)', contrary to its [ZFA counterpart](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ZFA_0005499) which is a material '[anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ZFA_0000037)'.

The text definition says it is a “fine ridge on surface of scale”, which suggests to me that ZFA is right in making it a material structure. If it is a “ridge”, then it must have some consistence. ",

file.content:
{
  "body": "A few of the root unsatisfiable classes found in the merge product of Uberon and ZFA are caused by what I believe are simple errors that are entirely internal to Uberon (and that are merely revealed by the merge with ZFA).

# uvea

[uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) is said to be [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) the '[anterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001801)', which is defined as “any of the parts of the eye that lie in front of, or ventral to, the lens (inclusive)”.

That doesn’t sound correct as, from what I can see, the uvea clearly appears to extend dorsally relative to the lens (if only by its '[optic choroid](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001776)' part, which is incidentally said to [overlap](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002131) the '[posterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001802).

The [uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some '[anterior segment of eyeball](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001801)' axiom should be removed – possibly replaced by a relationship to a larger structure, though this may not be needed since there is already axiom stating that [uvea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0001768) '[contributes to the morphology of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002433)' some '[camera-type eye](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000019)', which should be enough.

# 'future brain vesicle'

'[future brain vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013150)' (no text definition) is classified as an '[open anatomical space](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0010064)', ultimately an immaterial anatomical entity.

That does not seem consistent with Uberon’s “habits” of representing most anatomical cavities or spaces with terms representing the (material) structures that make them (plus optionally terms to represent the actual space inside, if it is needed for some reason).

'[future brain vesicle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013150)' is [part_of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) the '[ventricular system of central nervous system](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0005281)', which is explicitly defined as “a set of **structures** containing cerebrospinal fluid in the brain” (emphasis mine).

I believe the brain vesicles should be made to be material anatomical structures rather than immaterial anatomical spaces. If needed, we could create a new term “lumen of brain vesicle” to refer to the actual space within those vesicles, similarly to '[lumen of central canal of spinal cord](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0009572)'.

# 'scale circulus'

'[scale circulus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002051)' is classified as an immaterial '[anatomical line](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_2002051)', contrary to its [ZFA counterpart](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ZFA_0005499) which is a material '[anatomical structure](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/ZFA_0000037)'.

The text definition says it is a “fine ridge on surface of scale”, which suggests to me that ZFA is right in making it a material structure. If it is a “ridge”, then it must have some consistence. ",

    {
      "id": "IC_kwDOAAidd86gsdQ5",

  "title": "ZFA/Uberon issues: simple errors in Uberon",

content.thinking:
This issue describes three problems that need to be fixed in UBERON:

1. **uvea (UBERON:0001768)** - has an incorrect `part_of` relationship to 'anterior segment of eyeball', when it should not have this relationship since uvea extends beyond the anterior segment (its optic choroid part overlaps the posterior segment).

2. **future brain vesicle (UBERON:0013150)** - is currently classified as an 'open anatomical space' (immaterial), but should be a material anatomical structure since it's part of the ventricular system which is defined as structures.

3. **scale circulus (UBERON:2002051)** - is classified as an immaterial 'anatomical line' but should be a material anatomical structure based on its definition ("fine ridge on surface of scale").

Let me start by investigating these terms in the ontology using the prescribed methods. I'll need to:
1. Check out each term using `obo-checkout.pl`
2. Examine their current definitions and relationships
3. Make the appropriate fixes
4. Check them back in using `obo-checkin.pl`
5. Reserialize the edit file
6. Commit the changes

Let me start by examining the first term.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. This reports three simple errors in UBERON that need to be corrected:

1. **uvea (UBERON:0001768)** - incorrectly has `part_of` relationship to 'anterior segment of eyeball' when it extends beyond that region
2. **future brain vesicle (UBERON:0013150)** - incorrectly classified as an immaterial 'open anatomical space' when it should be a material structure
3. **scale circulus (UBERON:2002051)** - incorrectly classified as an immaterial 'anatomical line' when it should be a material structure

Let me investigate each of these terms and make the necessary corrections.

id: UBERON:0013150
name: future brain vesicle
synonym: "brain vesicle" RELATED []
synonym: "early brain vesicle" EXACT []
synonym: "primary brain vesicle" NARROW []
synonym: "primitive brain vesicle" EXACT []
synonym: "secondary brain vesicle" NARROW []
xref: NCIT:C34259
xref: neuronames:2499
xref: SCTID:360409004
is_a: UBERON:0010064 {source="EHDAA2"} ! open anatomical space
relationship: part_of UBERON:0000922 ! embryo
relationship: part_of UBERON:0005281 {source="EHDAA2"} ! ventricular system of central nervous system
relationship: part_of UBERON:0006238 ! future brain
property_value: editor_note "consider adding all secondary vesicles (e.g. diencephalic; my/metencephalic. See http://www.dartmouth.edu/~rswenson/NeuroSci/chapter_4.html)." xsd:string
property_value: external_ontology_notes "Note that some sources may treat the term vesicle as the progenitor of the whole brain region; for example 'The retina develops directly from the neural tube as an outpouching from the diencephalic vesicle'" xsd:string

[Term]
id: UBERON:0013150
name: future brain vesicle
synonym: "brain vesicle" RELATED []
synonym: "early brain vesicle" EXACT []
synonym: "primary brain vesicle" NARROW []
synonym: "primitive brain vesicle" EXACT []
synonym: "secondary brain vesicle" NARROW []
xref: NCIT:C34259
xref: neuronames:2499
xref: SCTID:360409004
is_a: UBERON:0010064 {source="EHDAA2"} ! open anatomical space
relationship: part_of UBERON:0000922 ! embryo
relationship: part_of UBERON:0005281 {source="EHDAA2"} ! ventricular system of central nervous system
relationship: part_of UBERON:0006238 ! future brain
property_value: editor_note "consider adding all secondary vesicles (e.g. diencephalic; my/metencephalic. See http://www.dartmouth.edu/~rswenson/NeuroSci/chapter_4.html)." xsd:string
property_value: external_ontology_notes "Note that some sources may treat the term vesicle as the progenitor of the whole brain region; for example 'The retina develops directly from the neural tube as an outpouching from the diencephalic vesicle'" xsd:string

content.content:
format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"
default-namespace: uberon

remark: Aurelie Comte, Bill Bug, Catherine Leroy, Duncan Davidson and Trish Whetzel are also contributors. However their ORCIDs were not found.

content.thinking:
Good! I found all three terms. Let me analyze what I need to do:

1. **UBERON:0001768 (uvea)**: Currently has `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001801 ! anterior segment of eyeball`. This should be removed since the uvea extends beyond the anterior segment. The term already has `relationship: contributes_to_morphology_of UBERON:0000019 ! camera-type eye` which is sufficient.

2. **UBERON:0013150 (future brain vesicle)**: Currently has `is_a: UBERON:0010064 {source="EHDAA2"} ! open anatomical space`. This should be changed. It should be a material anatomical structure. Let me search for what would be the appropriate parent class.

3. **UBERON:2002051 (scale circulus)**: Currently has `is_a: UBERON:0006800 ! anatomical line`. This should be changed to a material structure. Let me check what anatomical line is and what would be better.

Let me look up these parent terms first to better understand what to change them to.

content.text: Perfect! I've found all three terms. Now let me examine the parent classes to determine the correct replacements.

[Term]
id: UBERON:0010064
name: open anatomical space
def: "An anatomical space with at least one opening to another space or the exterior." [AEO:0000221]
xref: AEO:0000221
xref: EHDAA2:0004616
intersection_of: UBERON:0000464 ! anatomical space
intersection_of: has_quality PATO:0000610 ! open
relationship: connected_to UBERON:0000464 {source="cjm"} ! anatomical space

content.content:
format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"

id: UBERON:0006800
name: anatomical line
def: "Non-material anatomical entity of one dimension, which forms a boundary of an anatomical surface or is a modulation of an anatomical surface." [http://orcid.org/0000-0001-9114-8737]

format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"

synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"

synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"

synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"

property_value: dc-description "Uberon is an integrated cross-species anatomy ontology representing a variety of entities classified according to traditional anatomical criteria such as structure, function and developmental lineage. The ontology includes comprehensive relationships to taxon-specific anatomical ontologies, allowing integration of functional, phenotype and expression data." xsd:string

subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."

[Term]
id: UBERON:0000483
name: epithelium
def: "Portion of tissue, that consists of one or more layers of epithelial cells connected to each other by cell junctions and which is underlain by a basal lamina. Examples: simple squamous epithelium, glandular cuboidal epithelium, transitional epithelium, myoepithelium[CARO]." [http://orcid.org/0000-0001-9114-8737, Wikipedia:Epithelium]
comment: Editor note: surface epithelium may contain non-epithelial cells, such as melanocytes, lymphocytes and dendritic cells, within the sheet of epithelial cells. Do we consider these part of the epithelium, or located-in it?
subset: pheno_slim
subset: uberon_slim
subset: vertebrate_core
synonym: "epithelial tissue" EXACT []
synonym: "portion of epithelium" EXACT [FMA:9639]

property_value: depiction "https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/8f/Illu_epithelium.jpg" xsd:anyURI
property_value: has_relational_adjective "epithelial" xsd:string
property_value: homology_notes "The two basic types of metazoan tissue are epithelial and connective. The simplest metazoans, and developmental stages of many primitive invertebrates, consist solely of these two layers. Thus, epithelial and connective tissues may be the primary (original) tissues of metazoans, and both are important in the functional organization of animals.[well established][VHOG]" xsd:string {date_retrieved="2012-09-17", external_class="VHOG:0000387", ontology="VHOG", source="ISBN:978-0030259821 Ruppert EE, Fox RS, Barnes RD, Invertebrate zoology: a functional evolutionary approach (2003) p.59", source="http://bgee.unil.ch/"}

[Term]
id: UBERON:0000483
name: epithelium
def: "Portion of tissue, that consists of one or more layers of epithelial cells connected to each other by cell junctions and which is underlain by a basal lamina. Examples: simple squamous epithelium, glandular cuboidal epithelium, transitional epithelium, myoepithelium[CARO]." [http://orcid.org/0000-0001-9114-8737, Wikipedia:Epithelium]
comment: Editor note: surface epithelium may contain non-epithelial cells, such as melanocytes, lymphocytes and dendritic cells, within the sheet of epithelial cells. Do we consider these part of the epithelium, or located-in it?
subset: pheno_slim
subset: uberon_slim
subset: vertebrate_core
synonym: "epithelial tissue" EXACT []
synonym: "portion of epithelium" EXACT [FMA:9639]

property_value: homology_notes "The two basic types of metazoan tissue are epithelial and connective. The simplest metazoans, and developmental stages of many primitive invertebrates, consist solely of these two layers. Thus, epithelial and connective tissues may be the primary (original) tissues of metazoans, and both are important in the functional organization of animals.[well established][VHOG]" xsd:string {date_retrieved="2012-09-17", external_class="VHOG:0000387", ontology="VHOG", source="ISBN:978-0030259821 Ruppert EE, Fox RS, Barnes RD, Invertebrate zoology: a functional evolutionary approach (2003) p.59", source="http://bgee.unil.ch/"}

content.thinking:
Good! Now I understand the context better:

1. **UBERON:0010064 (open anatomical space)** - is an immaterial anatomical space
2. **UBERON:0006800 (anatomical line)** - is a non-material anatomical boundary
3. **UBERON:0000483 (epithelium)** - is tissue

For the fixes:

1. **UBERON:0001768 (uvea)**: Remove the `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001801 ! anterior segment of eyeball` line

2. **UBERON:0013150 (future brain vesicle)**: Need to find an appropriate material anatomical structure parent. Let me look for what other brain structures or vesicles use.

3. **UBERON:2002051 (scale circulus)**: Need to change from `is_a: UBERON:0006800 ! anatomical line` to a material structure. Since it's a "fine ridge on surface of scale" and is part_of dermal scale, it should probably be an anatomical structure. Let me search for what would be appropriate.

Let me look for anatomical structure or ridge-like terms:

content.text: Now let me search for appropriate parent terms for the fixes needed.

subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."

subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"

[Term]
id: UBERON:0000061
name: anatomical structure
def: "Material anatomical entity that is a single connected structure with inherent 3D shape generated by coordinated expression of the organism's own genome." [CARO:0000003]
subset: common_anatomy
subset: upper_level
synonym: "biological structure" EXACT []
synonym: "connected biological structure" EXACT [CARO:0000003]

tool_use_result.stdout:
format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"

format-version: 1.2
subsetdef: added_for_HCA "Classes tagged with this subset property were added upon request from the Human Cell Atlas (HCA)."
subsetdef: common_anatomy "Terms applicable across life, not restricted to Metazoa"
subsetdef: cumbo "CUMBO"
subsetdef: cyclostome_subset "cyclostome structures"
subsetdef: defined_by_cytoarchitecture "brain region defined by cyto-architecture"
subsetdef: defined_by_ordinal_series "classes that are defined by relative position counting from first in a series of elements along an axis in an individual organism rather than by strict homology"
subsetdef: developmental_classification "developmental_classification"
subsetdef: dubious_grouping "Indicates that subclasses in AOs may be inappropriately linked"
subsetdef: early_development "A class that represents an early developmental structure, like a blastocyst. This part of the ontology is undergoing review to remove inappropriate grouping classes."
subsetdef: efo_slim "EFO slim"
subsetdef: emapa_ehdaa2 "derived from the union of EHDAA2 and EMAPA - still to be checked"
subsetdef: feed_aligned "subset of classes that have logical defs text defs and synonyms aligned with FEED"
subsetdef: functional_classification "functional_classification"
subsetdef: grouping_class "Subset consisting of classes creating for grouping purposes"
subsetdef: homology_grouping "A grouping class that depends on an assumption of homology between subclasses"
subsetdef: human_reference_atlas ""
subsetdef: inconsistent_with_fma "classes that have some inconsistency with FMA"
subsetdef: location_grouping ""
subsetdef: major_organ "somewhat fuzzy grouping for analysis purposes, currently composed of something like: liver, heart, skeletal, kidney, bladder, brain, skin, mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestines, trachea nose, lungs, brain, spinal cord,  peripheral nerves, kidneys, ureters, bladder, urethra, gonads"
subsetdef: metazoa_core "core classes typically found across metazoa. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new metazoan AO. Note this subset is not yet fully fleshed out"
subsetdef: non_informative "abstract class brought in to group ontology classes but not informative"
subsetdef: organ_slim "organs, excluding individual muscles and skeletal elements"
subsetdef: pheno_slim "Phenotype slim"
subsetdef: phenotype_rcn "A subset specifically created for the 2012 Phenotype RCN meeting. Includes some human-specific terms that may eventually be removed when they are adequately represented with part of relationships in FMA"
subsetdef: uberon_slim "Uberon slim - subset that excludes obscure terms and deep compositional terms"
subsetdef: unverified_taxonomic_grouping "Indicates that the class encompasses classes from other AOs that have not been verified"
subsetdef: upper_level "abstract upper-level terms not directly useful for analysis"
subsetdef: vertebrate_core "core classes typically found across vertebrates. one purpose is to create a rough set of terms that could be used to start a new vertebrate AO"
synonymtypedef: BRAIN_NAME_ABV "brain name abbreviations (drosophila)"
synonymtypedef: COMPARATIVE_PREFERRED "preferred term in the context of comparative anatomy"
synonymtypedef: DEPRECATED "A historic synonym, no longer encouraged"
synonymtypedef: DEVELOPMENTAL "developmental term"
synonymtypedef: HOMOLOGY "a synonym made on the basis of a possibly homologous structure in another species"
synonymtypedef: HUMAN_PREFERRED "preferred term when talking about an instance of this class in Homo sapiens"
synonymtypedef: INCONSISTENT "indicates that a synonym is used in an inconsistent or confusing way, typically between species"
synonymtypedef: MISSPELLING "a mis-spelling that is in common use and thus recorded"
synonymtypedef: NON_AMNIOTE "synonym that is inappropriate for amniotes"
synonymtypedef: NON_MAMMAL "synonym that is inappropriate for mammals"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003000 "abbreviation"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003002 "dubious synonym"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003004 "plural form"
synonymtypedef: OMO:0003011 "latin term"
synonymtypedef: PENDING_REVIEW "expert consultation and attribution required"
synonymtypedef: SENSU "taxonomic disambiguation"
synonymtypedef: SYSTEMATIC "a systematic synonym used as the base name for design patterns"
default-namespace: uberon
treat-xrefs-as-equivalent: AEO
treat-xrefs-as-equivalent: BILA
treat-xrefs-as-equivalent: BSPO

Fix three simple errors identified in issue #3354

This commit fixes three ontology errors that were causing unsatisfiable 
classes in the merge product of Uberon and ZFA:

1. UBERON:0001768 (uvea): Removed incorrect 'part_of anterior segment of 
   eyeball' relationship. The uvea extends beyond the anterior segment (its 
   optic choroid part overlaps the posterior segment), so this relationship 
   was anatomically incorrect. The term retains 'contributes_to_morphology_of 
   camera-type eye' which is sufficient.

2. UBERON:0013150 (future brain vesicle): Changed classification from 
   'open anatomical space' (immaterial) to 'anatomical structure' (material). 
   This is consistent with it being part_of the ventricular system which is 
   explicitly defined as "a set of structures" (material entities).

3. UBERON:2002051 (scale circulus): Changed classification from 'anatomical 
   line' (immaterial) to 'anatomical structure' (material). The definition 
   states it is a "fine ridge on surface of scale", which has physical 
   substance and should be material, consistent with its ZFA counterpart.

These changes resolve logical inconsistencies and align Uberon with proper 
anatomical classifications.

Fixes #3354

@dragon-ai-agent
EOF
)"

input.content:
# Fix Simple Errors in Uberon (Issue #3354)

## Summary
This PR corrects three simple ontology errors that were causing unsatisfiable classes in the merge product of Uberon and ZFA.

## Changes Made

### 1. UBERON:0001768 (uvea)
**Problem**: The term had an incorrect `part_of` relationship to 'anterior segment of eyeball' (UBERON:0001801).

**Fix**: Removed the `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001801` axiom.

**Rationale**: The uvea clearly extends beyond the anterior segment. Specifically, its 'optic choroid' component (UBERON:0001776) is documented to overlap with the posterior segment of the eyeball (UBERON:0001802). The anterior segment is defined as structures "in front of, or ventral to, the lens (inclusive)", but the uvea extends dorsally relative to the lens. The term retains the `contributes_to_morphology_of camera-type eye` relationship, which is sufficient and correct.

### 2. UBERON:0013150 (future brain vesicle)
**Problem**: The term was classified as `is_a: UBERON:0010064` (open anatomical space), making it an immaterial entity.

**Fix**: Changed classification to `is_a: UBERON:0000061` (anatomical structure), making it a material entity.

**Rationale**: Brain vesicles are material structures, not immaterial spaces. This is consistent with:
- The term's `part_of` relationship to the ventricular system (UBERON:0005281), which is explicitly defined as "a set of **structures** containing cerebrospinal fluid" (emphasis on structures, not spaces)
- Standard anatomical conventions where cavities/spaces are represented by the material structures that form them
- If needed in the future, a separate term like "lumen of brain vesicle" could be created for the actual space within, similar to existing patterns in the ontology

### 3. UBERON:2002051 (scale circulus)
**Problem**: The term was classified as `is_a: UBERON:0006800` (anatomical line), making it an immaterial entity.

**Fix**: Changed classification to `is_a: UBERON:0000061` (anatomical structure), making it a material entity.

**Rationale**: The definition describes it as a "fine ridge on surface of scale, often parallel to the circumference of scale." A ridge has physical substance and mass, making it a material structure. This also brings Uberon into alignment with ZFA (ZFA:0005499), where scale circulus is correctly classified as a material anatomical structure.

## Validation
- All changes were made using the standard checkout/checkin workflow

tool_use_result.content:
# Fix Simple Errors in Uberon (Issue #3354)

## Summary
This PR corrects three simple ontology errors that were causing unsatisfiable classes in the merge product of Uberon and ZFA.

## Changes Made

### 1. UBERON:0001768 (uvea)
**Problem**: The term had an incorrect `part_of` relationship to 'anterior segment of eyeball' (UBERON:0001801).

**Fix**: Removed the `relationship: part_of UBERON:0001801` axiom.

**Rationale**: The uvea clearly extends beyond the anterior segment. Specifically, its 'optic choroid' component (UBERON:0001776) is documented to overlap with the posterior segment of the eyeball (UBERON:0001802). The anterior segment is defined as structures "in front of, or ventral to, the lens (inclusive)", but the uvea extends dorsally relative to the lens. The term retains the `contributes_to_morphology_of camera-type eye` relationship, which is sufficient and correct.

### 2. UBERON:0013150 (future brain vesicle)
**Problem**: The term was classified as `is_a: UBERON:0010064` (open anatomical space), making it an immaterial entity.

**Fix**: Changed classification to `is_a: UBERON:0000061` (anatomical structure), making it a material entity.

**Rationale**: Brain vesicles are material structures, not immaterial spaces. This is consistent with:
- The term's `part_of` relationship to the ventricular system (UBERON:0005281), which is explicitly defined as "a set of **structures** containing cerebrospinal fluid" (emphasis on structures, not spaces)
- Standard anatomical conventions where cavities/spaces are represented by the material structures that form them
- If needed in the future, a separate term like "lumen of brain vesicle" could be created for the actual space within, similar to existing patterns in the ontology

### 3. UBERON:2002051 (scale circulus)
**Problem**: The term was classified as `is_a: UBERON:0006800` (anatomical line), making it an immaterial entity.

**Fix**: Changed classification to `is_a: UBERON:0000061` (anatomical structure), making it a material entity.

**Rationale**: The definition describes it as a "fine ridge on surface of scale, often parallel to the circumference of scale." A ridge has physical substance and mass, making it a material structure. This also brings Uberon into alignment with ZFA (ZFA:0005499), where scale circulus is correctly classified as a material anatomical structure.

## Validation
- All changes were made using the standard checkout/checkin workflow
- Verified that the changes were correctly applied to src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo
- Confirmed removal of the problematic axioms through direct inspection

## Technical Details
- Used `obo-checkout.pl` to extract terms to `terms/` directory
- Made targeted edits to individual term files
- Used `obo-checkin.pl` to integrate changes back into main ontology

These fixes resolve logical inconsistencies that were preventing proper reasoning over the merged Uberon/ZFA ontology, and align the ontology with correct anatomical classifications.
